REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, OSC RE TURNOVER OF RECEIVERSHIP ESTATE PROPERTY. *RULING*: A HEARING IS SET FOR JUNE 12, 2025. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND DEFENDANT'S INVOICES ARE SUFFICIENT DOCUMENTATION OF "CONSISTENT MONTHLY BILLING" OR "CONTINUED ACCRUAL AND BILLING" FOR TWO REASONS. FIRST, DEFENDANT'S INVOICES ALL PRE-DATE THE WIRE TRANSFERS OF PREPAID RENTS FROM NOVEMBER 1, 2024 AND DECEMBER 30, 2024, WITH THE EXCEPTION OF ONE FEBRUARY 10, 2025 INVOICE. SECOND, DEFENDANT'S CEO ADMITTED THAT THE PREPAID RENTS WERE USED TO "OFFSET" PAYMENTS MADE IN 2024 FROM DEFENDANT TO PLAINTIFF, WHICH CREDIBLY SUGGESTS DEFENDANT KEPT THE PREPAID RENTS THAT WERE REQUIRED TO BE TURNED OVER TO THE RECEIVER. AS SUCH, DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE AN EXPLANATION AND ACCOUNTING, SUPPORTED BY UP-TO-DATE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS, LEDGERS, INVOICES, OR OTHER SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION, AS TO HOW THE MONEY FROM THE NOVEMBER 1, 2024 AND DECEMBER 30, 2024 WIRE TRANSFERS WAS SPENT. DEFENDANT IS FURTHER ORDERED TO PROVIDE A DECLARATION, SUPPORTED BY UP-TO-DATE DOCUMENTATION, ACCOUNTING THE EXPENDITURES MADE TO HARTFORD INSURANCE, PACIFIC WORKPLACES, JEFFER MANGELS, AND VERILIANT. DEFENDANT'S EXPLANATION AND ACCOUNTING MUST BE PROVIDED TO THE RECEIVER BY MAY 29, 2025. BY JUNE, 5, 2025, THE RECEIVER SHALL SUBMIT A REPORT TO THE COURT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT'S ACCOUNTING IS SATISFACTORY AND WHETHER THE JUNE 12, 2025 HEARING IS NECESSARY. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: VICTOR M. HWANG ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

OSC RE TURNOVER OF RECEIVERSHIP ESTATE PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

A hearing is set for June 12, 2025. The Court does not find Defendant's invoices are sufficient documentation of "consistent monthly billing" or "continued accrual and billing" for two reasons. First, Defendant's invoices all pre-date the wire transfers of prepaid rents from November 1, 2024 and December 30, 2024, with the exception of one February 10, 2025 invoice. Second, Defendant's CEO admitted that the prepaid rents were used to "offset" payments made in 2024 from Defendant to Plaintiff, which credibly suggests Defendant kept the prepaid rents that were required to be turned over to the Receiver. As such, Defendant is ordered to provide an explanation and accounting, supported by up-to-date financial statements, ledgers, invoices, or other supporting documentation, as to how the money from the November 1, 2024 and December 30, 2024 wire transfers was spent. Defendant is further ordered to provide a declaration, supported by up-to-date documentation, accounting the expenditures made to Hartford Insurance, Pacific Workplaces, Jeffer Mangels, and Veriliant. Defendant's explanation and accounting must be provided to the Receiver by May 29, 2025. By June, 5, 2025, the Receiver shall submit a report to the Court regarding whether Defendant's accounting is satisfactory and whether the June 12, 2025 hearing is necessary.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Victor M. Hwang ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
